content: 这是一个判断题的样例。答案为T，分值为5分。
author: 杨子祺
description: During the Merge Sort process, the order of magnitude of the number of comparisons is independent of the initial state of the sequence.

title: When sorting the array `{1, 13, 19, 10, 2, 5, 7, 4, 8, 20, 0, 15
content: When sorting the array ```{1, 13, 19, 10, 2, 5, 7, 4, 8, 20, 0, 15, 3, 11, 17}``` in ascending order by Shell Sort using Hibbard’s increments ($h_k=2^k-1)$, the sorting result after the first run is ```{1, 8, 19, 0, 2, 3, 7, 4, 13, 20, 10, 15, 5, 11, 17}``` and that after the second run is ```{0, 2, 3, 1, 4, 13, 5, 8, 15, 7, 10, 17, 20, 11, 19}``` .
author: 干红华
description: When sorting the array ```{1, 13, 19, 10, 2, 5, 7, 4, 8, 20, 0, 15, 3, 11, 17}``` in ascending order by Shell Sort using Hibbard’s increments ($h_k=2^k-1)$, the sorting result after the first run is ```{1, 8, 19, 0, 2, 3, 7, 4, 13, 20, 10, 15, 5, 11, 17}``` and that after the second run is ```{0, 2, 3, 1, 4, 13, 5, 8, 15, 7, 10, 17, 20, 11, 19}``` .

title: To sort { 8, 3, 9, 11, 2, 1, 4, 7, 5, 10, 6 } by Shell Sort, if
content:
To sort { 8, 3, 9, 11, 2, 1, 4, 7, 5, 10, 6 } by Shell Sort, if we obtain ( 4, 2, 1, 8, 3, 5, 10, 6, 9, 11, 7 ) after the first run, and ( 1, 2, 3, 5, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 10 ) after the second run, then the increments of these two runs must be __ , respectively.  @[](3)

A. 3 and 1
B. 3 and 2
C. 5 and 2
D. 5 and 3
author: 何钦铭
description: To sort { 8, 3, 9, 11, 2, 1, 4, 7, 5, 10, 6 } by Shell Sort, if we obtain ( 4, 2, 1, 8, 3, 5, 10, 6, 9, 11, 7 ) after the first run, and ( 1, 2, 3, 5, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 10 ) after the second run, then the increments of these two runs must be __ , respectively.

title: To sort $$N$$ elements by heap sort, the extra space complexity
content:
To sort $$N$$ elements by heap sort, the extra space complexity is: @[](1)

A. $$O(1)$$
B. $$O(logN)$$
C. $$O(N)$$
D. $$O(NlogN)$$

title: To sort $$N$$ records by merge sort, the worst-case time complex
content:
To sort $$N$$ records by merge sort, the worst-case time complexity is: @[](1)

A. $$O(logN)$$
B. $$O(N)$$
C. $$O(NlogN)$$
D. $$O(N^2)$$

author: DS课程组

title: Insertion or Heap Sort
content: According to Wikipedia: Insertion sort iterates, consuming one input element each repetition, and growing a sorted output list. Each iteration, insertion sort removes one element from the input data, finds the location it belongs within the sorted list, and inserts it there. It repeats until no input elements remain. Heap sort divides its input into a sorted and an unsorted region, and it iteratively shrinks the unsorted region by extracting the largest element and moving that to the sorted region. it involves the use of a heap data structure rather than a linear-time search to find the maximum. Now given the initial sequence of integers, together with a sequence which is a result of several iterations of some sorting method, can you tell which sorting method we are using? ### Input Specification: Each input file contains one test case. For each case, the first line gives a positive integer $$N$$ ($$\le 100$$). Then in the next line, $$N$$ integers are given as the initial sequence. The last line contains the partially sorted sequence of the $$N$$ numbers. It is assumed that the target sequence is always non-decreasing. All the numbers in a line are separated by a space. ### Output Specification: For each test case, print in the first line either "Insertion Sort" or "Heap Sort" to indicate the method used to obtain the partial result. Then run this method for one more iteration and output in the second line the resulting sequence. It is guaranteed that the answer is unique for each test case. All the numbers in a line must be separated by a space, and there must be no extra space at the end of the line. ### Sample Input 1: ```in 10 3 1 2 8 7 5 9 4 6 0 1 2 3 7 8 5 9 4 6 0 ``` ### Sample Output 1: ```out Insertion Sort 1 2 3 5 7 8 9 4 6 0 ``` ### Sample Input 2: ``` 10 3 1 2 8 7 5 9 4 6 0 6 4 5 1 0 3 2 7 8 9 ``` ### Sample Output 2: ``` Heap Sort 5 4 3 1 0 2 6 7 8 9 ``` **鸣谢徐杨锦晖同学修正题面！**
author: 陈越
description: According to Wikipedia: Insertion sort iterates, consuming one input element each repetition, and growing a sorted output list. Each iteration, insertion sort removes one element from the input data, finds the location it belongs within the sorted list, and inserts it there. It repeats until no input elements remain. Heap sort divides its input into a sorted and an unsorted region, and it iteratively shrinks the unsorted region by extracting the largest element and moving that to the sorted region. it involves the use of a heap data structure rather than a linear-time search to find the maximum. Now given the initial sequence of integers, together with a sequence which is a result of several iterations of some sorting method, can you tell which sorting method we are using? ### Input Specification: Each input file contains one test case. For each case, the first line gives a positive integer $$N$$ ($$\le 100$$). Then in the next line, $$N$$ integers are given as the initial sequence. The last line contains the partially sorted sequence of the $$N$$ numbers. It is assumed that the target sequence is always non-decreasing. All the numbers in a line are separated by a space. ### Output Specification: For each test case, print in the first line either "Insertion Sort" or "Heap Sort" to indicate the method used to obtain the partial result. Then run this method for one more iteration and output in the second line the resulting sequence. It is guaranteed that the answer is unique for each test case. All the numbers in a line must be separated by a space, and there must be no extra space at the end of the line. ### Sample Input 1: ```in 10 3 1 2 8 7 5 9 4 6 0 1 2 3 7 8 5 9 4 6 0 ``` ### Sample Output 1: ```out Insertion Sort 1 2 3 5 7 8 9 4 6 0 ``` ### Sample Input 2: ``` 10 3 1 2 8 7 5 9 4 6 0 6 4 5 1 0 3 2 7 8 9 ``` ### Sample Output 2: ``` Heap Sort 5 4 3 1 0 2 6 7 8 9 ``` **鸣谢徐杨锦晖同学修正题面！**
